html:
[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 读书不数过，即能背诵 - Literal translation of 'reading a book a few times and being able to recite it,' indicating exceptional memory and intelligence.
  • 尤喜出新意，作诸技巧 - '尤喜' means 'especially likes,' and '出新意' means 'to come up with new ideas.' '作诸技巧' refers to making various ingenious devices.
  • 七八岁时，尝背塾师，暗窃匠氏刀锥，凿木人长寸许，置案上能自行走，手足皆自动，观者异以为神 - '尝背塾师' means 'once, behind the back of his school teacher,' and '暗窃匠氏刀锥' means 'secretly stole tools from a craftsman.' '凿木人长寸许' means 'carved a wooden figure about an inch long,' and '置案上能自行走，手足皆自动' describes the figure walking and moving its limbs on its own. '观者异以为神' means 'spectators were amazed, thinking it was a miracle.'
  • 十岁外，先姑父弃世，来广陵，与予同居 - '十岁外' means 'after ten years of age,' and '先姑父弃世' means 'his maternal uncle had passed away.' '来广陵，与予同居' means 'he came to Guangling and lived with me.'
  • 因闻泰西几何比例、轮捩机轴之学，而其巧因以益进 - '泰西' refers to the West, '几何比例' means 'geometric proportions,' and '轮捩机轴之学' refers to mechanical engineering. '而其巧因以益进' means 'and his skill advanced further.'
  • 尝作小物自怡，见者多竞出重价求购 - '尝作小物自怡' means 'he often made small objects for his own amusement,' and '见者多竞出重价求购' means 'those who saw them offered high prices to buy them.'
  • 体素病，不耐人事，恶剧嬲，因竟不作，于是所制始不可多得 - '体素病' means 'he was always sick,' and '不耐人事' means 'could not tolerate social interactions.' '恶剧嬲' means 'hated being bothered by others,' and '因竟不作' means 'so he stopped making things.' '于是所制始不可多得' means 'and thus his creations became rare.'
  • 所制亦多，予不能悉记 - '所制亦多' means 'he made many things,' and '予不能悉记' means 'I cannot remember all of them.'
  • 犹记其解双轮小车一辆，长三尺许，约可坐一人，不烦推挽能自行 - '犹记其解双轮小车一辆' means 'I still remember one of his inventions, a two-wheeled cart,' and '长三尺许' means 'about three feet long.' '约可坐一人' means 'it could accommodate one person,' and '不烦推挽能自行' means 'it could move on its own without needing to be pushed or pulled.'
  • 行住，以手挽轴旁曲拐，则复行如初 - '行住' means 'when it stops,' and '以手挽轴旁曲拐' means 'by pulling the curved lever on the axle.' '则复行如初' means 'it would start moving again as before.'
  • 随住随挽，日足行八十里 - '随住随挽' means 'whenever it stops, pull the lever,' and '日足行八十里' means 'it could travel eighty miles in a day.'
  • 作木狗，置门侧，卷卧如常，唯人入户，触机则立吠不止 - '作木狗' means 'he made a wooden dog,' and '置门侧' means 'placed it by the door.' '卷卧如常' means 'it lay curled up as usual,' and '唯人入户，触机则立吠不止' means 'but when someone entered the door, it would stand up and bark continuously.'
  • 吠之声与真无二，虽黠者不能辨其为真与伪也 - '吠之声与真无二' means 'the sound of its barking was indistinguishable from a real dog,' and '虽黠者不能辨其为真与伪也' means 'even the most clever people could not tell if it was real or fake.'
  • 作木鸟，置竹笼中，能自跳舞飞鸣，鸣如画眉，凄越可听 - '作木鸟' means 'he made a wooden bird,' and '置竹笼中' means 'placed it in a bamboo cage.' '能自跳舞飞鸣' means 'it could dance, fly, and sing on its own,' and '鸣如画眉，凄越可听' means 'its song sounded like a mandarin duck, clear and melodious.'
  • 作水器，以水置器中，水从下上射如线，高五六尺，移时不断 - '作水器' means 'he made a water device,' and '以水置器中' means 'he placed water in the device.' '水从下上射如线' means 'the water shot up like a line,' and '高五六尺，移时不断' means 'reaching five or six feet high and continuing for a while.'
  • 所作之奇俱如此，不能悉载 - '所作之奇俱如此' means 'all of his creations were like this,' and '不能悉载' means 'they cannot all be described.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄子履庄 - A historical figure known for his mechanical inventions.
  • 泰西 - Refers to the West, particularly the European countries that were known for their advanced technology during this period.
  • 几何比例、轮捩机轴之学 - These terms refer to the study of geometry and mechanics, which were introduced to China from the West.
  • 广陵 - An ancient name for Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄子履庄 - A proper name for the inventor.
  • 广陵 - An ancient name for Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有怪其奇者 - Someone who finds it strange that it is so extraordinary.
  • 或有异传 - Or perhaps there is an unusual tradition.
  • 绝不见其书 - Never sees any books.
  • 叩其从来 - To inquire about its origin.
  • 何足奇 - What is there to be surprised about?
  • 天地人物，皆奇器也 - Heaven, earth, human beings, and all things are all extraordinary mechanisms.
  • 动者如天，静者如地，灵明者如人，赜者如万物 - Those in motion are like heaven, those at rest are like earth, those with consciousness are like humans, and those complex are like all things.
  • 何莫非奇 - Nothing is not extraordinary.
  • 然皆不能自奇 - But they cannot make themselves extraordinary.
  • 必有一至奇而不自奇者以为源 - There must be an extremely extraordinary one that does not make itself extraordinary as the source.
  • 而且为之主宰 - And serves as the master or controller.
  • 如画之有师，土木之有匠氏也 - Like paintings having a teacher, and earth and wood having craftsmen.
  • 黄子之奇，必得其奇之所以然乎 - The extraordinariness of Mr. Huang must come from understanding the reason behind extraordinariness.
  • 性简默 - Nature is simple and taciturn.
  • 喜思 - Likes to think.
  • 戛戛似难 - It seems very difficult.
  • 思得，则笑舞从之 - When he thinks of something, he laughs and dances following it.
  • 拥衾达旦 - Sleeps with his quilt until dawn.
  • 务得而后已焉 - Determined to achieve it and only then will he stop.
  • 黄子之奇，固亦由思而得之者也 - The extraordinariness of Mr. Huang indeed comes from thinking.
  • 而其喜思则性出也 - And his liking to think comes from his nature.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄子 - Refers to a person named Huang, whose extraordinariness is being discussed.
  • 天地人物，皆奇器也 - This reflects a philosophical view that everything in the universe has its own extraordinary nature.
  • 画之有师，土木之有匠氏也 - This metaphor suggests that just as art and craftsmanship have masters, so too does the extraordinariness of the universe have a source.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄子 - The name of the person being discussed.

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 毫发无异 - Means 'identical down to the smallest detail.'
  • 验器冷热燥湿 - Refers to a device that measures temperature and moisture levels, detectable only by touch.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丙申 - A year in the Chinese zodiac cycle, corresponding to 1866 in the Gregorian calendar.
  • 验器 - Refers to scientific or technological devices, possibly early inventions for measurement or observation.
  • 镜 - Mirrors with various functions, reflecting technological or artistic advancements.
  • 画 - Chinese paintings used for decoration, observation, or artistic expression.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄子 - A personal name, likely referring to a historical or literary figure.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旁視畫 - Literal descriptive phrase meaning 'side-view painting,' likely referring to a specific type of visual representation.
  • 管窥鏡畫 - Refers to a painting viewed through a tube, suggesting a unique perspective or effect.
  • 全不似畫 - Means 'not at all like a painting,' indicating that the painting appears more like something else when viewed through the tube.
  • 以管窥之 - Means 'to view it through a tube,' emphasizing the method of observation.
  • 動如真 - Means 'as if it were real,' describing the lifelike appearance of the painting when viewed through the tube.
  • 上下畫 - Refers to a painting that appears different when viewed from above and below.
  • 一畫上下觀之 - Means 'to view the same painting from above and below,' highlighting the dual perspective.
  • 三面畫 - Refers to a painting that appears different when viewed from three sides.
  • 一畫三面觀之 - Means 'to view the same painting from three sides,' emphasizing the multi-perspective nature.
  • 玩器器雖玩而理則誠 - A complex phrase meaning 'playthings, though played with, are grounded in truth,' suggesting that even toys have a deeper, genuine aspect.
  • 玩以理出 - Means 'play arises from reason,' indicating that play is rooted in rationality.
  • 君子亦無廢乎玩矣 - Means 'gentlemen also do not abandon play,' suggesting that even wise individuals engage in play.
  • 自動戲 - Refers to an automatic toy or game, likely involving mechanisms that operate without external intervention.
  • 內音樂俱備 - Means 'with internal music,' indicating that the toy has built-in musical elements.
  • 不煩人力 - Means 'without the need for human effort,' suggesting that the toy operates autonomously.
  • 而節奏自然 - Means 'and the rhythm is natural,' describing the smooth and natural operation of the toy.
  • 真畫 - Refers to a lifelike painting, likely one that appears very realistic.
  • 人物鳥獸，皆能自動 - Means 'figures and animals can all move on their own,' indicating a highly advanced form of animation.
  • 與真無二 - Means 'no different from reality,' emphasizing the lifelike quality of the painting.
  • 燈衢 - Refers to a lantern-lit street or market, likely a miniature or illusionary representation.
  • 作小屋一间 - Means 'to make a small house,' indicating the creation of a miniature structure.
  • 内悬灯数盏 - Means 'with several lamps hanging inside,' describing the interior of the small house.
  • 人入其中 - Means 'when people enter it,' suggesting an interactive experience.
  • 如至通衢大市 - Means 'as if they have arrived at a bustling market,' describing the illusion of being in a lively place.
  • 人烟稠杂 - Means 'crowded with people,' emphasizing the crowded nature of the scene.
  • 灯火连绵 - Means 'with lights stretching continuously,' describing the continuous illumination.
  • 一望数里 - Means 'visible for several miles,' indicating the expansive view.
  • 自行驱暑扇 - Refers to an automatic fan that cools the room, likely a mechanical device.
  • 木人掌扇 - Refers to a wooden figure holding a fan, likely a mechanical or animated figure.
  • 水法农必借水而成 - Means 'the method of water is essential for farming,' indicating the importance of water in agriculture.
  • 水之用大矣 - Means 'the use of water is very great,' emphasizing the significance of water.
  • 而亦可为诸玩 - Means 'and it can also be used for various playthings,' suggesting that water can be used in recreational devices.
  • 作水器 - Means 'to make water devices,' indicating the creation of water-related objects.
  • 龙尾车 - Refers to a dragon-tail cart, likely a mechanical device used for irrigation.
  • 一人能转多车 - Means 'one person can turn multiple carts,' indicating the efficiency of the device.
  • 灌田最便 - Means 'the most convenient for irrigating fields,' emphasizing the practicality of the device.
  • 一线泉 - Refers to a stream of water, likely a small, continuous flow.
  • 制法不等 - Means 'methods of construction vary,' indicating that there are different ways to create such a stream.
  • 柳枝泉 - Refers to a water feature that resembles willow branches, likely a decorative or artistic representation.
  • 水上射复下 - Means 'water shoots up and falls down,' describing the movement of water.
  • 如柳枝然 - Means 'like willow branches,' emphasizing the resemblance.
  • 山鸟鸣 - Refers to the sound of birds in the mountains, likely a sound effect in a device.
  • 声如山鸟 - Means 'the sound is like that of mountain birds,' describing the quality of the sound.
  • 鸾凤吟 - Refers to the song of phoenixes, likely a sound effect in a device.
  • 声如鸾凤 - Means 'the sound is like that of phoenixes,' describing the quality of the sound.
  • 报时水 - Refers to a water clock that tells time, likely a mechanical device.
  • 瀑布水 - Refers to a waterfall, likely a water feature or sound effect.
  • 工欲善其事，必先利其器 - A well-known saying meaning 'a craftsman must sharpen his tools if he wants to do his job well,' emphasizing the importance of preparation.
  • 况目中所列诸器 - Means 'especially the various devices mentioned in the text,' indicating that the saying applies specifically to the devices discussed.
  • 有非寻常斤斧所能造者 - Means 'some of which cannot be made with ordinary tools,' suggesting that some devices require specialized skills or materials.
  • 作造器之器 - Means 'to make devices for making other devices,' indicating the creation of tools or machinery.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 旁視畫 - A specific type of visual representation that may have been popular in certain historical contexts.
  • 管窥鏡畫 - A unique form of painting that suggests a specialized technique or perspective.
  • 自动戲 - An early form of mechanical or automated entertainment, reflecting the technological advancements of the time.
  • 真画 - A highly advanced form of painting that may have been considered a marvel of art and technology.
  • 燈衢 - A miniature or illusionary representation of a bustling market, reflecting the cultural fascination with creating realistic scenes.
  • 自行驱暑扇 - An early form of mechanical cooling device, reflecting the ingenuity of ancient engineers.
  • 木人掌扇 - A mechanical or animated figure, reflecting the development of automata.
  • 水法农必借水而成 - The importance of water in agriculture, reflecting the agricultural practices of the time.
  • 龙尾车 - A mechanical device used for irrigation, reflecting the practical needs of farmers.
  • 一线泉 - A small, continuous flow of water, reflecting the aesthetic appreciation of natural elements.
  • 柳枝泉 - A decorative or artistic representation of water, reflecting the cultural value placed on beauty and artistry.
  • 山鸟鸣 - The sound of birds in the mountains, reflecting the natural soundscape and the desire to replicate it in devices.
  • 鸾凤吟 - The song of phoenixes, reflecting the mythological and symbolic significance of these birds in Chinese culture.
  • 报时水 - A water clock, reflecting the importance of timekeeping in ancient societies.
  • 瀑布水 - A waterfall, reflecting the natural beauty and the desire to recreate it in devices.
  • 工欲善其事，必先利其器 - A well-known saying that emphasizes the importance of preparation and the right tools, reflecting a broader cultural value.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 方圆规矩 - Refers to the traditional Chinese emphasis on order and precision in craftsmanship and art.
  • 就小画大规矩 - Refers to the method of using smaller patterns or templates to create larger designs.
  • 就大画小规矩 - Refers to the method of using larger patterns or templates to create smaller designs.
  • 画八角六角规矩 - Refers to the specific methods for drawing shapes with eight or six sides, emphasizing the precision required.
  • 造诸镜规矩 - Refers to the rules or techniques for making various types of mirrors.
  • 造法条器 - Refers to the creation of legal documents or instruments.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泰西人 - Refers to Westerners, particularly those from Europe, known for their technological and scientific advancements during the Ming and Qing dynasties.
  • 关帝君祠 - Refers to the Temple of Guan Yu, a popular site for worship in China, dedicated to the historical figure Guan Yu, who was deified and revered as a god of loyalty and righteousness.
  • 江阴城陷 - Refers to the fall of Jiangyin, a significant event in Chinese history, often associated with resistance against foreign invasions or internal conflicts.

Proper Nouns

  • 张山来 - Zhang Shanlai, a commentator or scholar mentioned in the text.
  • 梅子定九 - Mei Ziding, a skilled individual mentioned in the text.
  • 吴子师邵 - Wu Shishao, another skilled individual mentioned in the text.
  • 黄子履庄 - Huang Zilu, a third skilled individual mentioned in the text.
  • 虞初新志 - The title of the historical account.
  • 周亮工 - Zhou Lianggong, the author of the historical account.
  • 江阴 - Jiangyin, a city in China.
  • 戚三郎 - Qi Sanlang, a historical figure mentioned in the text.
  • 王笃 - Wang Du, the wife of Qi Sanlang.
  • 关帝君祠 - The Temple of Guan Yu.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 戚心独朗朗 - Describes the protagonist's heart as being clear and bright, indicating a state of purity or clarity of mind.
  • 念虔事帝 - Means 'thinking devoutly about serving the emperor,' suggesting a sincere and reverent attitude towards imperial service.
  • 得死楹下足矣 - Literally means 'to die under the pillar would be enough,' expressing a willingness to sacrifice oneself for a higher cause.
  • 然度难死 - Means 'but considering it difficult to die,' indicating a realization that death is not easily achieved.
  • 帝显赫，或有以援我 - Translates to 'the emperor is majestic, perhaps someone will help me,' suggesting a hope for divine or imperial intervention.
  • 日且暮，觉祠中有异 - Means 'as the day was ending, he felt something strange in the temple,' describing a sense of unease or supernatural occurrence.
  • 纠臂带忽裂，裂声如弓弦，作霹雳鸣 - Describes the sudden breaking of a bandage with a sound like a bowstring snapping, followed by a thunderclap, indicating a dramatic and possibly supernatural event.
  • 戚臂左受创，纠缚既断，因得以右扶首 - Means 'Qiqi's left arm was injured, the bandage broke, and he could then use his right hand to support his head,' describing a physical struggle and a moment of relief.
  • 首将堕，喉固未绝，因宛转正之 - Translates to 'his head was about to fall, but his throat was not yet cut, so he turned and straightened it,' indicating a critical moment of survival.
  • 心朗朗，念帝显赫，真援我也 - Means 'his heart was clear, thinking of the emperor's majesty, truly helping me,' expressing gratitude and a sense of divine assistance.
  • 黎明，兵数过戚 - Means 'at dawn, soldiers passed by Qiqi,' describing the early morning scene.
  • 见血痕模糊，谓死矣，不复顾 - Translates to 'seeing the bloody traces, they thought he was dead and did not look back,' indicating the soldiers' assumption of his death.
  • 久之，有老翁妪趋视戚 - Means 'after a while, an old man and woman hurried to see Qiqi,' describing the arrival of neighbors.
  • 怜之曰：“三郎垂毙矣，盍掖之归？” - Translates to 'they pitied him and said, 'Young Master Qiqi is on the verge of death, why don't we carry him home?'' expressing concern and a desire to help.
  • 戚虽愦然，心识其为比邻钱翁、沈妪也 - Means 'Qiqi was dazed, but he recognized them as his neighbors, Old Master Qian and Old Lady Shen,' indicating a moment of recognition.
  • 顷之，两人续以姜糜至 - Translates to 'soon, the two brought porridge with ginger to him,' describing the neighbors' care.
  • 越二日，入曰：“兵封刃，行且去，郎活矣！”乃不复至 - Means 'after two days, they came in and said, 'The soldiers have sealed their weapons and are about to leave, you are alive!'' indicating the end of danger.
  • 戚首为血糨，乃因之固，渐能起 - Translates to 'Qiqi's head was bandaged with blood, and he gradually regained strength and could stand up,' describing his recovery.
  • 举视室中，无一存者，五龄儿固坐足旁泣 - Means 'he looked around the room and saw no one else, only a five-year-old child sitting beside his feet and crying,' indicating the desolation of the scene.
  • 而屋中乃僵二尸，辨之，邻钱翁、沈妪也 - Translates to 'in the house, there were two stiff bodies, which he recognized as his neighbors, Old Master Qian and Old Lady Shen,' indicating the tragic fate of the neighbors.
  • 戚恐甚，久之，悟两人殆关帝命以援予者 - Means 'Qiqi was very afraid, but after a while, he realized that the two must have been sent by the Lord Guan to help him,' expressing a sense of divine intervention.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 关帝 - Refers to Lord Guan (Guan Yu), a revered military general and deity in Chinese culture, known for his loyalty and righteousness.
  • 祠 - Refers to a temple or shrine, often dedicated to a deity or ancestor.
  • 纠臂带 - Refers to a bandage or binding used to secure an injured limb.
  • 楹 - Refers to a pillar or column in a building, often used in temples or halls.

Proper Nouns

  • 戚 - The protagonist's name, Qiqi.
  • 关帝 - Lord Guan (Guan Yu), a revered deity in Chinese culture.
  • 钱翁 - Old Master Qian, a neighbor.
  • 沈妪 - Old Lady Shen, a neighbor.

[216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因强起 - Literally 'forced to get up,' indicating a reluctant or painful action.
  • 跋躄 - A term describing a limping or unsteady gait, often used to depict physical weakness or injury.
  • 叩首状 - A gesture resembling prostration or bowing, traditionally a sign of deep respect or submission.
  • 首又若将离者 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that the head seems about to detach, emphasizing extreme weakness or pain.
  • 依槛祝曰 - Literally 'leaning on the railing and praying,' indicating a moment of supplication or plea.
  • 身赖帝活 - A statement of dependence on the 'Emperor' (likely a deity or divine figure) for survival.
  • 唯帝终有以庇予 - A plea for continued protection or shelter from the 'Emperor.'
  • 翁妪死而生我 - A statement that the narrator was born after the death of their grandparents.
  • 可为槥 - Refers to the use of wood to make a simple coffin.
  • 第安所得匠 - A rhetorical question expressing the difficulty of finding a craftsman.
  • 往迹之 - Literally 'went to look for them,' indicating a search for the craftsmen.
  • 踦户语 - A description of the craftsmen leaning against the door and talking.
  • 戚告以故 - The narrator explained the situation to the craftsmen.
  • 咸随戚归 - The craftsmen all followed the narrator home.
  • 失三匠而存五槥 - The narrator lost three of the craftsmen and was left with five coffins.
  • 去又不俟予归耶 - A rhetorical question expressing frustration or confusion about the craftsmen leaving without waiting for the narrator.
  • 窅无人 - Describes the emptiness or desolation of the place, likely the 'Emperor's' palace.
  • 三尸仆户内外 - Describes the corpses of the three craftsmen lying inside and outside the door.
  • 倩所识 - Literally 'entrusted to someone known,' indicating that the narrator asked a familiar person to help.
  • 厝翁妪及匠 - Refers to the act of burying the grandparents and the craftsmen.
  • 瘗之隙地 - Describes the act of burying the bodies in a secluded or hidden place.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 帝祠 - Refers to a shrine or temple dedicated to a deity or divine figure, likely the 'Emperor' mentioned in the text.
  • 槥 - A simple coffin, often used in ancient China for burial.
  • 三匠踦户语 - The craftsmen are described as leaning against the door and talking, which may reflect a common posture or social interaction in the context of the time.
  • 空室得冬炒半囊归 - The narrator finds a partially full bag of 'winter stir-fry' in an empty room, suggesting a scene of poverty or scarcity.
  • 隙地 - A secluded or hidden place, often used for burial in ancient Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 帝 - Refers to a deity or divine figure, likely the 'Emperor' mentioned in the text.
  • 翁妪 - Refers to the grandparents of the narrator.
  • 匠 - Refers to the craftsmen who helped the narrator make the coffins.
  • 槥 - Refers to the simple coffins made by the craftsmen.

[217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 神理亦漸旺 - A phrase indicating that the spiritual essence or divine power is gradually becoming stronger.
  • 稽首投地 - A ritual gesture of deep respect or worship, involving bowing with the head touching the ground.
  • 帝恩我無極 - An expression of deep gratitude, meaning 'the emperor's grace to me is boundless.'
  • 第妻無由見 - A phrase expressing the inability to see one's wife, with '第' indicating a contrast or concession.
  • 帝其以夢示 - A request for the emperor to reveal something through a dream.
  • 辨明，力疾負子行至津亭 - A phrase describing the act of clearly distinguishing, enduring illness, carrying one's child, and walking to a ferry station.
  • 有舟待 - Indicates that a boat is waiting.
  • 越月之十四日 - Refers to a specific date in the future, 'on the fourteenth day of the month.'
  • 終不可見矣 - A statement that the meeting will ultimately not happen.
  • 帝示我矣 - A phrase meaning 'the emperor has shown me.'
  • 為此子觅母 - Means 'to find a mother for this child.'
  • 附舟行 - Indicates traveling by boat.
  • 帝佑君，合浦珠自当还 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that the emperor's protection will ensure the return of something valuable, like a pearl from Hépǔ.
  • 借君庇以分神贶 - Means 'to borrow your protection to share the divine favor.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The exclamation '天乎！安所得金' is a direct expression of frustration, which should be preserved in the translation.
  • The sequence '戚乃大哭，妇哭，而戚子又趢{走豕}往来，哭于父母旁' describes multiple people crying in different ways, which should be rendered clearly in English.
  • The extended description '郝哭，张之厮养哭，张姬妾环屏内者亦哭，久之，张亦涔涔泪下矣' should be broken into clear, sequential statements in English.
  • The sudden action of Zhang standing up amidst the chaos '哭声鼎沸间，张突跃起曰' should be clearly conveyed.
  • The repetition of '止止' emphasizes the urgency of stopping the situation.
  • The cause-and-effect relationship between the fall of the city and the lack of money '城陷家破，尔诚无所得金' should be preserved.
  • The explanation for survival '且尔数被创弗死，非帝祐，不至是' should be translated clearly, emphasizing the divine protection.
  • The conditional statement about returning the wife '尔诚善者，吾还尔妇，不须金也' should be translated accurately.
  • The complex conditional and moral reasoning '成以尔故售身于吾，尔夫妇还而成留，成即不怨尔，尔何以谢成' should be broken down into clear, sequential statements.
  • The offer to return the wife and her friends' wives '吾即还尔妇，兼还尔友夫妇' should be translated clearly.
  • The instruction for the couple to return with their friends' couples '尔夫妇其与尔友夫妇俱还' should be translated accurately.
  • The final statement about the money '此二十金，即为尔辈道里需，不须金也' should be translated clearly, explaining its purpose.

  • 已渐稠密 - 已 means 'already,' 渐 means 'gradually,' and 稠密 means 'dense.' It indicates that the lights are becoming more numerous and dense.

  • 有并携二灯者 - 有 means 'there are,' 并 means 'together,' 携 means 'carrying,' and 二灯 means 'two lamps.' This phrase describes someone carrying two lamps together.

This complex phrase describes a group of many lamps arranged in a line, moving slowly, as if officials are going out with a retinue leading the way.
  • 有若二队相值，各分去者 - 有 means 'there are,' 若 means 'as if,' 二队 means 'two groups,' 相值 means 'meeting,' 各分去 means 'separating and going different ways.' This phrase describes two groups meeting and then separating to go different ways.
  • 有相值若揖若语而别者 - 有 means 'there are,' 相值 means 'meeting,' 若 means 'as if,' 揖 means 'bowing,' 语 means 'speaking,' and 别 means 'parting.' This phrase describes two groups meeting, bowing, speaking, and then parting.
  • 有高擎者，有下移者 - 有 means 'there are,' 高擎 means 'held high,' and 下移 means 'moved down.' This phrase describes some lamps being held high and others being moved down.
  • 有置灯憩坐者 - 有 means 'there are,' 置灯 means 'placing the lamp,' and 憩坐 means 'resting.' This phrase describes someone placing the lamp and resting.
  • 有穿林踏险而行者 - 有 means 'there are,' 穿林 means 'passing through the forest,' 踏险 means 'stepping on dangerous ground,' and 而行 means 'and moving.' This phrase describes someone passing through the forest, stepping on dangerous ground, and moving.
  • 有渡江者，始渡若揭衣踌躇，登岸则速者 - 有 means 'there are,' 渡江 means 'crossing the river,' 始渡 means 'beginning to cross,' 若 means 'as if,' 揭衣 means 'lifting the clothes,' 踌躇 means 'hesitating,' and 登岸则速 means 'quickly reaching the shore.' This phrase describes someone crossing the river, hesitating at the beginning, and then quickly reaching the shore.
  • 其光或颓若有所幪 - 其光 means 'the light,' 或 means 'sometimes,' 颓 means 'dim,' 若 means 'as if,' and 有所幪 means 'as if covered.' This phrase describes the light sometimes dimming as if covered.
  • 或光动若庭燎 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 光动 means 'the light moving,' 若 means 'as if,' and 庭燎 means 'lanterns in the courtyard.' This phrase describes the light sometimes moving as if like lanterns in a courtyard.
  • 或灭或复明 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 灭 means 'extinguished,' and 复明 means 'reigniting.' This phrase describes the light sometimes going out and then reigniting.
  • 或数灯合为一，或一分为数 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 数灯 means 'several lamps,' 合为一 means 'combining into one,' 或 means 'sometimes,' 一分为数 means 'splitting into several.' This phrase describes several lamps sometimes combining into one and sometimes splitting into several.
  • 或迎风疾行，焰反向而炽 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 迎风 means 'against the wind,' 疾行 means 'moving quickly,' 焰 means 'flame,' 反向 means 'opposite direction,' and 炽 means 'burning brightly.' This phrase describes the flame sometimes moving quickly against the wind, burning brightly in the opposite direction.
  • 或徐行则敛，或驻则渐微 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 徐行 means 'moving slowly,' 则 means 'then,' 敛 means 'becoming less bright,' 或 means 'sometimes,' 驻 means 'stopping,' and 渐微 means 'gradually dimming.' This phrase describes the light sometimes becoming less bright when moving slowly and gradually dimming when stopping.
  • 或排列一线，若星桥灯市 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 排列 means 'arranged in a line,' 一线 means 'a line,' 若 means 'as if,' 星桥 means 'star bridge,' and 灯市 means 'lantern market.' This phrase describes the lights sometimes arranged in a line, as if a star bridge or lantern market.
  • 或独燃幽处，若寒窗爇灯荧荧然 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 独燃 means 'burning alone,' 幽处 means 'in a secluded place,' 若 means 'as if,' 寒窗 means 'cold window,' 爇灯 means 'burning a lamp,' and 荧荧然 means 'flickering.' This phrase describes the light sometimes burning alone in a secluded place, as if a flickering lamp in a cold window.
  • 或高在山半若悬竿，或出江间丛苇中若渔火 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 高在山半 means 'high on the mountain,' 若 means 'as if,' 悬竿 means 'hanging from a pole,' 或 means 'sometimes,' 出江间 means 'coming out of the river,' 丛苇中 means 'among the reeds,' and 若渔火 means 'like fishing lights.' This phrase describes the light sometimes high on the mountain, as if hanging from a pole, and sometimes coming out of the river among the reeds, like fishing lights.
  • 或远，或近在数十步内 - 或 means 'sometimes,' 远 means 'far,' 或 means 'sometimes,' 近在 means 'close to,' and 数十步内 means 'within dozens of steps.' This phrase describes the light sometimes far away and sometimes close within dozens of steps.
  • 熟视灯下，若有二足影，喁喁若闻语声，而实无语 - 熟视 means 'to look closely,' 灯下 means 'under the light,' 若有 means 'as if there were,' 二足影 means 'two foot shadows,' 喁喁 means 'whispering,' 若闻语声 means 'as if hearing voices,' and 而实无语 means 'but actually no words.' This phrase describes looking closely under the light, as if seeing two foot shadows, hearing whispering voices, but actually no words.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 呜呼 - Interjection expressing sorrow or lamentation.
  • 洵神也 - Indeed, it is divine; '洵' emphasizes the truth or certainty of the statement.
  • 塞天地，贯古今 - Fills the heavens and earth, runs through ancient and modern times; hyperbolic expression indicating omnipresence and eternal existence.
  • 无乎不在 - Is nowhere absent; present everywhere.
  • 必姚江，必东郊，必四三月，必热将雨始见 - Specific conditions under which the phenomenon is observed; emphasizes specificity.
  • 探赜索隐 - To explore the profound and seek the hidden; pursuit of deep understanding.
  • 采传闻、览怪志 - To gather hearsay and examine strange records; collection of various accounts.
  • 疑惑聚讼 - To be full of doubts and disputes; state of uncertainty and disagreement.
  • 目所经见 - What the eyes have witnessed; emphasizes personal observation.
  • 久立凝睇 - To stand for a long time with fixed gaze; prolonged attention.
  • 不知所由然 - To not know why it happens; confusion about the cause.
  • 求为博物君子 - To seek to be a learned gentleman; '博物君子' refers to someone knowledgeable in many fields.
  • 不可知 - Unknown; beyond human comprehension.
  • 质世之多闻者 - To present to the world's many-knowing ones; seek verification from those with extensive knowledge.
  • 其年丙戌，其月癸巳，其日己卯 - Specific dates in the Chinese sexagenary cycle; used to record the exact time of the event.
  • 同游者 - Those who traveled together; companions on the journey.
  • 年友 - Yearly friend; term of respect for someone of the same age or social standing.
  • 门下士 - Scholar in the school; student or follower.
  • 焚之而鬼不至 - Act of burning the document and the failure of the spirits to appear.
  • 旧板刷文重读 - Act of using the old boards to re-read the text.
  • 燐火复炽 - Phosphorescent lights reigniting.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 穿窬之盗 - A classical idiom referring to a thief who digs through walls or climbs through windows, often used to describe a petty thief.
  • 豪侠之盗 - Refers to a thief with a sense of chivalry or romanticism, often associated with the '侠' (knight-errant) archetype.
  • 斩关辟门 - Literally 'cutting through gates and opening doors,' used to describe a thief who boldly and forcefully breaks into places.
  • 贪婪无厌 - Means 'greedy and insatiable,' describing someone who is never satisfied.
  • 冒死不顾 - Means 'recklessly disregarding death,' describing someone who is willing to risk their life.
  • 从容坐论 - Means 'calmly discussing while seated,' suggesting a composed and intellectual demeanor.
  • 杯酒欢笑 - Refers to drinking and laughing together, often used to describe a friendly or convivial atmosphere.
  • 名士 - Refers to a person of high moral and cultural standing, often associated with the literati class.
  • 迫于饥寒 - Means 'driven by hunger and cold,' describing someone who is forced to act out of necessity.
  • 为仇恶报怨 - Means 'to seek revenge for grudges,' describing someone who acts out of a desire for retribution.
  • 不得已而为之 - Means 'acting out of necessity,' describing a situation where someone has no other choice.
  • 盗而名士 - Refers to a thief who is also a person of high moral and cultural standing, a rare and unusual combination.
  • 家世为显官 - Means 'born into a family of high-ranking officials,' describing someone from a prestigious background.
  • 厚其赀 - Means 'having a substantial fortune,' describing someone who is wealthy.
  • 庾于田 - Means 'engaged in agriculture,' describing someone involved in farming.
  • 挈其爱妾 - Means 'taking his beloved concubine,' describing a man bringing his favored female companion.
  • 刈于乡之别墅 - Means 'reaping at a country villa,' describing the act of harvesting crops at a rural estate.
  • 自屋而下 - Means 'descending from the house,' describing someone coming down from a building.
  • 启其户 - Means 'opening the door,' describing the act of entering a place.
  • 连进十数辈 - Means 'a dozen or so people entering in succession,' describing a series of people entering one after another.
  • 萧先生睡耶 - Means 'Is Mr. Xiao asleep?' a question asked to check if someone is awake.
  • 就榻促之起 - Means 'urging him to get up from the bed,' describing someone being prompted to rise.
  • 为先生着衣裳 - Means 'dressing Mr. Xiao in clothes,' describing the act of getting someone dressed.
  • 进冠履 - Means 'putting on a hat and shoes,' describing the act of dressing someone formally.
  • 若执僮仆役 - Means 'as if performing the duties of a servant,' describing someone acting in a subservient manner.
  • 甚谨 - Means 'very careful,' describing someone who is meticulous.
  • 键其室 - Means 'locking his room,' describing the act of securing a space.
  • 设坐，面南向 - Means 'setting up a seat facing south,' describing the arrangement of a seating position.
  • 爇烛其下 - Means 'lighting a candle below,' describing the act of illuminating a space.
  • 某读先生今古文 - Means 'I have read Mr. Xiao's modern and ancient writings,' describing someone who has studied the works of another.
  • 可一一为先生诵之 - Means 'I can recite them for Mr. Xiao one by one,' describing the act of reciting something aloud.
  • 最佳者无如某篇 - Means 'the best is without a doubt a certain piece,' describing the highest quality work.
  • 某篇之中，有某转某句 - Means 'in a certain piece, there is a certain turn of phrase,' describing a specific part of a work.
  • 非巧思不能道 - Means 'only with clever thought can it be expressed,' describing the complexity of a piece of writing.
  • 尝于某显曹处私伺先生宴 - Means 'I once secretly observed Mr. Xiao's banquet at a certain high-ranking official's residence,' describing someone who has witnessed a private event.
  • 连饮十五犀觥 - Means 'drinking fifteen cups of wine in succession,' describing someone who has consumed a large amount of alcohol.
  • 诸公不及也 - Means 'the others could not match him,' describing someone's exceptional ability.
  • 江南藩司碑记 - Refers to the memorial inscriptions of the Jiangnan provincial administration, describing official records or monuments.
  • 唯先生文为绝笔 - Means 'only Mr. Xiao's writing is unparalleled,' describing the unique quality of someone's work.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 左右有恐吓先生者 - 有人在左右恐吓先生，其中‘左右’指身边的人，‘恐吓’指威胁或恐吓。
  • 其盗魁力止之 - 盗贼首领极力阻止，‘盗魁’指盗贼的首领，‘力止之’指极力阻止。
  • 此萧先生，不可以常态惊也 - 萧先生不是普通人，不能用平常的方式对待，‘不可以常态惊也’指萧先生不是普通人。
  • 索酒肴相啖食 - 要酒和菜肴一起吃，‘索酒肴’指要酒和菜肴，‘相啖食’指互相吃喝。
  • 先生为之陈庖厨 - 展示厨艺或准备食物，‘陈庖厨’指展示厨艺或准备食物。
  • 饮酣 - 喝酒喝得尽兴，‘饮酣’指喝酒喝得尽兴。
  • 某等闻先生名久矣 - 我们早就听说过先生的大名，‘某等’指我们这些人，‘闻先生名久矣’指我们早就听说过先生的大名。
  • 不惜千金路费至此 - 不惜花费大量金钱来到这里，‘不惜千金路费’指不惜花费大量金钱。
  • 可出其囊橐以偿吾愿 - 可以拿出钱袋满足我们的愿望，‘囊橐’指钱袋，‘偿吾愿’指满足我们的愿望。
  • 昨有四百金稻谷价，惜来迟耳 - 昨天有四百金的稻谷钱，可惜来晚了，‘惜来迟耳’指可惜来晚了。
  • 此所留者，仅羹酒之需，不过二十七金，人参八两，玉带一围而已 - 剩下的只有二十七两银子、八两人参和一围玉带，‘仅羹酒之需’指仅仅是为了吃喝，‘不过二十七金’指只有二十七两银子。
  • 愿持赠诸豪士 - 愿意赠送各位豪杰，‘愿持赠’指愿意赠送，‘诸豪士’指各位豪杰。
  • 左右疑有埋藏者 - 怀疑有隐藏的东西，‘疑有埋藏者’指怀疑有隐藏的东西。
  • 此先生真实语也，不须疑 - 这是先生真实的话，不必怀疑，‘真实语’指真实的话，‘不须疑’指不必怀疑。
  • 启其箧，如数 - 打开箱子，数量相符，‘启其箧’指打开箱子，‘如数’指数量相符。
  • 夜将半，先生倦，且恐 - 半夜时先生疲倦且害怕，‘夜将半’指半夜，‘倦’指疲倦，‘且恐’指而且害怕。
  • 我为先生起舞 - 我为先生跳舞，‘我为先生起舞’指我为先生跳舞。
  • 解长服，甲铠绣鲜，金光灿耀夺人目 - 脱下长袍，铠甲上绣有鲜艳的图案，金光闪闪，耀眼夺目，‘甲铠绣鲜’指铠甲上绣有鲜艳的图案，‘金光灿耀夺人目’指金光闪闪，耀眼夺目。
  • 拔双剑，起舞厅中 - 拔出两把剑，在厅中跳舞，‘拔双剑’指拔出两把剑，‘起舞厅中’指在厅中跳舞。
  • 往来近先生鼻端 - 舞动时剑几乎碰到先生的鼻子，‘往来近先生鼻端’指舞动时剑几乎碰到先生的鼻子。
  • 迹其状，如项庄鸿门意在沛公时也 - 看他的样子，像项庄在鸿门宴上意在刺杀沛公时一样，‘迹其状’指看他的样子，‘如项庄鸿门意在沛公时也’指像项庄在鸿门宴上意在刺杀沛公时一样。
  • 良久乃止 - 很久才停下来，‘良久乃止’指很久才停下来。
  • 先生待益恭，盗益重先生 - 先生对待更加恭敬，盗贼更加尊重先生，‘待益恭’指对待更加恭敬，‘盗益重先生’指盗贼更加尊重先生。
  • 自启户论文，始终敬礼先生，卒不敢犯如此 - 从开门讨论文章，始终尊敬先生，最终不敢冒犯，‘自启户论文’指从开门讨论文章，‘始终敬礼先生’指始终尊敬先生，‘卒不敢犯如此’指最终不敢冒犯。

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 萧先生 - 指萧先生，具体身份不详，但显然是一个有学问或有地位的人。
  • 盗魁 - 指盗贼的首领，通常指有组织的盗贼集团的头目。
  • 项庄鸿门意在沛公 - 指项庄在鸿门宴上意在刺杀沛公（刘邦）的历史典故，比喻表面上是友好的举动，实际上有恶意。
  • 千金路费 - 指花费大量金钱的路费，古代‘千金’常用来形容大笔财富。

Proper Nouns

  • 萧先生 - 指萧先生，具体身份不详。
  • 盗魁 - 指盗贼的首领。
  • 项庄 - 指项庄，历史人物，鸿门宴上的重要人物。
  • 沛公 - 指沛公，即刘邦，汉朝的开国皇帝。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先生房委曲，四顧夜黑 - Describes the setting: the room is cramped, and the surroundings are dark. '委曲' means cramped or narrow, and '四顧夜黑' suggests a dim, enclosed space.
  • 持灯周书幌曰 - Means 'holding a lamp, he examined the window curtains and said.' '持灯' means holding a lamp, and '周书幌' means examining the window curtains.
  • 此窗棂宜向某处上下，此楼宜对某方 - Means 'this window frame should be adjusted up and down, this tower should face a certain direction.' '宜' means should or ought to, and '上下' and '对某方' suggest adjustments in position and orientation.
  • 所惜鸠工时少经营耳 - Means 'what is regrettable is that the craftsmen were not well managed during construction.' '鸠工' means to gather workers, and '少经营' means poorly managed or neglected.
  • 窥先生藏书 - Means 'he looked into Mr. X's library.' '窥' means to look into or inspect.
  • 见《名臣奏议》《忠臣谱》二集 - Means 'he saw two collections of memorials by famous ministers and a collection of loyal ministers.' '名臣奏议' refers to memorials written by famous ministers, and '忠臣谱' refers to a collection of loyal ministers.
  • 乌用此玷辱书斋？ - Means 'why would you use this to defile the study?' '乌用' is an interrogative particle, and '玷辱' means to defile or disgrace.
  • 择其不佳者毁裂之 - Means 'he chose the poor ones and tore them up.' '不佳' means poor or inferior, and '毁裂' means to tear up or destroy.
  • 有美人一幅，乃名笔 - Means 'there was a painting of a beautiful woman, which was a famous work.' '美人' means a beautiful woman, and '名笔' means a famous work of art.
  • 此不可多觏者 - Means 'this is not something one can often see.' '不可多觏' means not often seen.
  • 罗君某写有小楷扇一柄，藏笔床侧 - Means 'Mr. Luo had a small calligraphy fan, which was kept beside the writing desk.' '小楷扇' means a small calligraphy fan, and '笔床侧' means beside the writing desk.
  • 吾与此公有旧好，宜珍之 - Means 'I have an old friendship with this gentleman, so it should be treasured.' '旧好' means old friendship, and '宜珍之' means should be treasured.
  • 将出门，邀先生送 - Means 'as he was about to leave, he invited Mr. X to see him off.' '邀' means to invite, and '送' means to see off.
  • 先生强留曰 - Means 'Mr. X tried to keep him, saying.' '强留' means to try to keep, and '曰' means to say.
  • 若辈皆少年豪侠，待至明日归取四百金相遗何如？ - Means 'you all are young heroes, why don't you wait until tomorrow to take 400 gold pieces as a gift?' '若辈' means you all, '少年豪侠' means young heroes, '待至明日' means wait until tomorrow, '归取' means to take, '四百金' means 400 gold pieces, and '相遗' means to give as a gift.
  • 盗魁曰 - Means 'the leader of the thieves said.' '盗魁' means the leader of the thieves.
  • 世从无其事，余何能待？ - Means 'such things never happen in the world, how can I wait?' '世从无其事' means such things never happen, and '余何能待' means how can I wait.
  • 请姓名，不答 - Means 'he asked for his name, but he did not respond.' '请' means to ask, '姓名' means name, and '不答' means did not respond.
  • 后会有期 - Means 'we will meet again.' '后会' means meet again, and '有期' means there is a time.
  • 惜先生老，若少壮，当与之同往 - Means 'I am sorry, Mr. X, you are old, if you were young, I would go with you.' '惜' means I am sorry, '老' means old, '若少壮' means if you were young, and '当与之同往' means I would go with you.
  • 先生出走里许 - Means 'Mr. X walked out about a li.' '出走' means walked out, and '里许' means about a li.
  • 见木舟二，泊溪日，尽登，摇橹而去 - Means 'he saw two wooden boats moored in the stream, he climbed aboard both, and rowed away.' '木舟' means wooden boats, '泊溪日' means moored in the stream, '尽登' means climbed aboard both, and '摇橹而去' means rowed away.
  • 语作吴下音 - Means 'he spoke in Wu dialect.' '吴下音' means Wu dialect.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先生 - Refers to a respected teacher or scholar.
  • 藏书 - Refers to a collection of books, often in a library or study.
  • 名臣奏议 - Refers to memorials written by famous ministers, which were official documents submitted to the emperor.
  • 忠臣谱 - Refers to a collection of loyal ministers, often compiled to honor those who served the state faithfully.
  • 小楷扇 - Refers to a small calligraphy fan, a common item in traditional Chinese culture, often used for both practical and aesthetic purposes.
  • 笔床侧 - Refers to the side of a writing desk, where writing tools and other items might be kept.
  • 吴下音 - Refers to the Wu dialect, a regional dialect spoken in the lower Yangtze River area, particularly in modern-day Jiangsu and Zhejiang provinces.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '先生房委曲，四顧夜黑，持灯周书幌曰' introduces the setting and action in a compact manner. '委曲' and '四顧夜黑' describe the room's cramped and dark conditions, while '持灯周书幌曰' describes the action of examining the window curtains.
  • The phrase '此窗棂宜向某处上下，此楼宜对某方' provides specific instructions for the arrangement of the window frame and the orientation of the tower. The structure is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the intended meaning.
  • The phrase '所惜鸠工时少经营耳' expresses regret over the poor management of the craftsmen during construction. The structure is straightforward but requires attention to the nuance of '少经营.'
  • The phrase '窥先生藏书，见《名臣奏议》《忠臣谱》二集' describes the act of looking into the library and seeing specific books. The structure is clear but requires attention to the specific titles mentioned.
  • The phrase '乌用此玷辱书斋？' is a rhetorical question expressing disapproval. The structure is clear but requires attention to the rhetorical nature of the question.
  • The phrase '择其不佳者毁裂之' describes the act of selecting and destroying poor-quality items. The structure is clear but requires attention to the specific action described.

  • 天下有道，将安其身于烟霞泉石之中乎 - Rhetorical question suggesting that if the world was governed by virtue, one would seek refuge in natural beauty rather than constantly interacting with others.
  • 皇皇如也欲与天下之士日相见哉 - Phrase describing the anxious desire to meet scholars every day, implying a sense of urgency or ambition.
  • 顾天有不可逆者，而终皭然长往矣 - Statement indicating that despite the heavens' unchangeable nature, one remains pure and continues on their path.
  • 凤集于棘，鷃雀调之 - Metaphor comparing the phoenix (凤) gathering on thorns (棘) with small birds (鷃雀) disturbing it, symbolizing the contrast between noble and base elements.
  • 神龙潜乎深渊，终能雨此九土也 - Metaphor suggesting that even when the divine dragon (神龙) hides in deep waters, it can still bring rain to all nine lands (九土), symbolizing latent power and eventual manifestation.
  • 张山来曰：古无神仙，非无神仙也。耕田凿井，含哺鼓腹，夫人而神仙也。古无异人，何以异于人哉？尧、舜与人同耳。然则神仙、异人之有，其于中古乎？读此可以知世变矣。 - Commentary by Zhang Shanlai explaining that ancient times did not lack immortals or extraordinary people, but rather that everyone could achieve a state of immortality through simple living and that extraordinary individuals were not fundamentally different from ordinary people.

  • 居数年，积钱盈室 - After several years, I had accumulated a lot of money.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 时姐夫为令于兖，往从之 - This sentence is straightforward, but the phrase '往从之' could be interpreted as 'I went to follow him' or 'I went to join him.'
  • 会姐夫出，姐见之，惊曰 - The sequence of events is clear, but the phrase '会姐夫出' could be interpreted as 'Just as my sister-in-law was about to leave' or 'When my sister-in-law left.'
  • 一足益恚恨，乃镌其梃曰 - The phrase '一足益恚恨' is a bit ambiguous, but it clearly means 'I became even more angry and resentful.'
  • 见渔舟数百泊市米，一足求载以济 - The phrase '一足求载以济' is straightforward but could be interpreted as 'I asked for a ride to cross the sea' or 'I asked for a ride to help me.'
  • 其地不立塾，晨令童子持一钱诣师，师书一字于掌教之，则童子揖而退，明日复来 - This long sequence must be read as a single description of the teaching method.

  • 髯有兄进香茅山，堕崖折胸死 - '髯有兄' means 'his brother,' '进香茅山' means 'went to Mount Mao to burn incense,' and '堕崖折胸死' means 'fell off a cliff and died with a broken chest.'
  • 或传其暮夜饮酒不诚，被王灵官鞭杀者 - '或传' means 'some say,' '暮夜饮酒不诚' means 'drank at night without sincerity,' and '被王灵官鞭杀者' means 'was beaten to death by the God of Wonders.'

  • 饮大醉，数王灵官曰 - '饮大醉' means 'drank heavily,' and '数王灵官曰' means 'rebuked the God of Wonders, saying.'
  • 汝有罪三 - '汝有罪三' means 'you have three sins.'
  • 人敬祖师，来进香，固有善心，饮酒小过，无死状，汝辄杀之，不仁，罪一 - '人敬祖师' means 'people respect the ancestral teachers,' '来进香' means 'come to burn incense,' '固有善心' means 'naturally have good intentions,' '饮酒小过' means 'drinking is a minor fault,' '无死状' means 'not deserving of death,' '汝辄杀之' means 'you killed him,' '不仁' means 'unjust,' and '罪一' means 'first sin.'
  • 祖师以慈庇下土，量甚宏大，汝居位下，行残忍，不遵祖师意，不恭，罪二 - '祖师以慈庇下土' means 'the ancestral teachers protect the world with kindness,' '量甚宏大' means 'with great tolerance,' '汝居位下' means 'you in your position,' '行残忍' means 'act cruelly,' '不遵祖师意' means 'do not follow the ancestral teachers' will,' '不恭' means 'disrespectful,' and '罪二' means 'second sin.'
  • 吾兄，小人也，酬香而来，小被酒，汝辄杀之；吾来不酬香，昨实大饮，今日詈汝，汝反不能杀，无勇，罪三 - '吾兄，小人也' means 'my brother, a commoner,' '酬香而来' means 'came to burn incense,' '小被酒' means 'slightly drunk,' '汝辄杀之' means 'you killed him,' '吾来不酬香' means 'I came without burning incense,' '昨实大饮' means 'yesterday I drank heavily,' '今日詈汝' means 'today I insult you,' '汝反不能杀' means 'you can't even kill me,' and '无勇' means 'lack of courage,' '罪三' means 'third sin.'
  • 汝宜毁撤，曷为横鞭嗔目，坐踞于此 - '汝宜毁撤' means 'you should be destroyed and removed,' '曷为' means 'why,' '横鞭嗔目' means 'brandish your whip and glare,' and '坐踞于此' means 'sit here.'

  • 取剑臂之，跨卫向南山径去 - She took a sword and placed it on her arm, then mounted the guard and headed towards the south mountain trail; '臂之' means 'placed on her arm' and '跨卫' means 'mounted the guard.'
  • 其行如飞，倏忽不见 - Her movement was as swift as flying, and she vanished in a flash; '倏忽' emphasizes the sudden disappearance.
  • 市人集观者数百人 - Hundreds of people gathered in the market to watch; '集观者' means 'gathered to watch.'
  • 比东归，再往访之，庵已空无人矣 - By the time I returned eastward, I went to visit again, but the nunnery was empty and no one was there; '比' means 'by the time' and '空无人矣' means 'was empty and no one was there.'
  • 尼高髻盛装，衣锦绮，行缠罗袜，年十八九好女子也 - The nun wore a high hairstyle and elaborate attire, dressed in brocade and silk, with her feet wrapped in fine gauze socks, and she was a beautiful woman in her late teens; '高髻盛装' describes her hairstyle and clothing, and '好女子' means 'beautiful woman.'
  • 市人云：“尼三四年前，挟妪俱来，不知何许人。常有恶少夜入其室，腰斩掷垣外，自是无敢犯者。” - The people in the market said, 'The nun came here with the old woman three or four years ago, and no one knows where she is from. Often, ruffians would sneak into her room at night, only to be beheaded and thrown over the wall; since then, no one dares to offend her'; '腰斩' means 'beheaded' and '掷垣外' means 'thrown over the wall.'

  • 南山 - Refers to the South Mountain, a common reference in classical Chinese literature, often symbolizing retreat or seclusion.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sequence of dialogue must be read as a coherent narrative, with the innkeeper providing information about the mysterious person and the narrator's decision to seek out the nun.
  • This sequence describes the nun's actions in a rapid, almost supernatural manner, emphasizing her extraordinary nature.
  • This sequence describes the aftermath of the nun's actions, with the people gathering to watch and the nun returning with the stolen goods.
  • This final sequence indicates the narrator's return and the nun's disappearance, leaving the nunnery empty.

  • The long sentence '使我为任生，则随毛女入深山中，亦效其饵女贞实，共作仙家眷属，何乐如之？计不出此，何也？' must be read as a single unit, with the conditional clause '使我为任生' leading to the scenario described in the main clause.

  • The sentence '一日者，旭始旦，一人急遽趋而来，至馆饭，饭毕，酬值，急遽趋而去' describes a sequence of events: a person hurriedly came, ate at the inn, paid the bill, and left hurriedly.

  • The sentence '生固辞。主人曰："吾勿暇，而不可却也。"' means the host firmly declined, but the owner said that he had no time but could not refuse.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 家居益与弟錝尽读所藏书 - Means 'at home, he and his brother Zhen read all the books they had collected.' '家居' refers to being at home, and '尽读' means to read thoroughly or exhaustively.
  • 尤善《易》学 - Indicates that he was particularly skilled in the study of the *Yi Jing* (Book of Changes). '尤善' means 'particularly good at' or 'especially proficient in.'
  • 而父汀州太守显、母恭人章，更时时慰勉之 - Means 'and his father, who was the governor of Tingzhou, and his mother, Lady Zhang, often comforted and encouraged him.' '慰勉' means to comfort and encourage.
  • 至父死，服阙，复起为兵部车驾司主事，分守皇城，益修城守火器 - Describes the sequence of events: 'When his father died, he completed his mourning period, and then he was appointed as a clerk in the Vehicle Department of the Ministry of War, responsible for guarding the imperial city, and he further improved the city's defenses and weapons.' '服阙' refers to completing the mourning period, and '复起' means to resume official duties.
  • 时崇祯十七年二月也 - Specifies the time: 'It was February of the seventeenth year of the Chongzhen era.' '崇祯' is the era name of Emperor Chongzhen, and '十七年' means the seventeenth year.
  • 李自成已陷大同 - Means 'Li Zicheng had already captured Datong.' '陷' means to capture or conquer.
  • 而宣府镇方有太监杜勋监视 - Means 'and at the same time, the eunuch Du Xun was overseeing the Xuanfu garrison.' '监视' means to supervise or oversee.
  • 又上疏曰 - Means 'He also submitted a memorial saying.' '上疏' means to submit a memorial or petition to the emperor.
  • 宣府京城之蔽，宣府不救，虑在京城 - Means 'Xuanfu is the shield of the capital city; if Xuanfu is not saved, the danger will be in the capital.' '蔽' means a shield or protection.
  • 抚臣朱之冯忠勇足恃，恐受内臣之掣，请亟撤之，并撤居庸关监视 - Means 'The governor, Zhu Zhi Feng, is loyal and brave enough to rely on, but I fear he will be hindered by the eunuchs. I request that he be immediately recalled, along with the supervisor at Juyong Pass.' '掣' means to hinder or obstruct, and '亟撤' means to urgently recall.
  • 不听 - Means 'The request was not heeded.' '不听' means to not listen or not grant.
  • 至三月，果闻杜勋以宣府迎贼，朱死之 - Means 'By March, it was indeed reported that Du Xun had welcomed the rebels into Xuanfu, and Zhu Zhi Feng died as a result.' '果闻' means to indeed hear or learn.
  • 因哭语弟錝 - Means 'He then wept and told his brother Zhen.' '哭语' means to weep and speak.
  • 目今我哭朱公，数日后汝曹旋哭我也 - Means 'Now I am weeping for Zhu Gong, but in a few days, you will be weeping for me.' '汝曹' means you all or you guys, and '旋' means soon or shortly.

  • 吾与若同游京师 - 吾 means 'I', 与 means 'with', 若 means 'you', 同游 means 'travel together', 京师 means 'the capital'. The phrase means 'I and you have traveled together in the capital.'
  • 未尝一争道角技 - 未尝 means 'never', 一 means 'once', 争道 means 'compete in skill', 角技 means 'compete in technique'. The phrase means 'we have never once competed in skill.'
  • 即诸先生何所用吾与若耶 - 即 means 'then', 诸先生 means 'all the sirs' or 'various masters', 何所用 means 'what use', 吾与若耶 means 'me and you'. The phrase means 'then what use are we, me and you?'
  • 今愿毕其所长 - 今 means 'now', 愿 means 'wish', 毕 means 'fully', 所长 means 'talents'. The phrase means 'now I wish to fully demonstrate my talents.'
  • 博诸先生欢 - 博 means 'win' or 'gain', 诸先生 means 'all the sirs' or 'various masters', 欢 means 'joy'. The phrase means 'to win the joy of all the sirs.'
  • 遂争出注，约百缗 - 遂 means 'therefore', 争出注 means 'competed to bet', 约百缗 means 'with a bet of a hundred strings of cash'. The phrase means 'therefore, they competed to bet with a bet of a hundred strings of cash.'
  • 百龄固谢不敢 - 百龄 is a proper name, 固 means 'firmly', 谢 means 'declined', 不敢 means 'dare not'. The phrase means 'Bai Ling firmly declined, saying he dared not.'
  • 林君益骄，益强之 - 林君 means 'Mr. Lin', 益 means 'more' or 'increasingly', 骄 means 'arrogant', 强之 means 'forced him'. The phrase means 'Mr. Lin became increasingly arrogant and forced him.'
  • 遂对弈 - 遂 means 'therefore', 对弈 means 'played chess'. The phrase means 'therefore, they played chess.'
  • 枰未半，林君面颊发赤热 - 枰 means 'chessboard', 未半 means 'not halfway', 林君 means 'Mr. Lin', 面颊 means 'cheeks', 发赤热 means 'turned red and hot'. The phrase means 'not halfway through the game, Mr. Lin's cheeks turned red and hot.'
  • 而百龄信手以应，旁若无人 - 而 means 'but', 百龄 is a proper name, 信手 means 'at will' or 'casually', 以应 means 'to respond', 旁若无人 means 'as if no one else were there'. The phrase means 'but Bai Ling responded casually, as if no one else were there.'
  • 凡三战，林君三北 - 凡 means 'total', 三战 means 'three battles', 林君 means 'Mr. Lin', 三北 means 'three defeats'. The phrase means 'in total three battles, Mr. Lin suffered three defeats.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甲申国亡 - Refers to the fall of the Ming Dynasty in 1644, marked by the Geng Shen year in the Chinese sexagenary cycle.
  • 父随卒 - Indicates the immediate aftermath of the nation's fall, with the father dying shortly after.
  • 人屋承父志 - The name 'Rén Wū' is a pun on '承父志' (carry on the father's will), indicating a strong familial and moral obligation.
  • 亦喑哑 - Suggests a physical or emotional trauma, becoming mute.
  • 左右承事者，皆语以目 - Those who served him on the left and right all communicated with him through gestures, indicating a form of non-verbal communication.
  • 合则颔之，否则摇头 - If he agreed, he would nod; if not, he would shake his head, describing a simple system of non-verbal responses.
  • 对宾客寒暄以手 - He greeted guests with gestures, emphasizing the use of gestures in social interactions.
  • 听人言古今事，心会处，则哑然笑 - When he heard people talk about ancient and modern events, if he understood them, he would laugh silently, describing a form of silent comprehension and appreciation.
  • 遂弃家为僧 - He then abandoned his family and became a monk, marking a significant change in his life.
  • 自号曰“雪个” - He gave himself the name 'Xue Ge' (Snow Piece), a poetic and symbolic choice, possibly reflecting a sense of purity or detachment.
  • 未几病颠 - Not long after, he fell ill and became deranged, indicating a mental health issue.
  • 伏地呜咽 - He would crouch on the ground and weep, describing a state of deep sorrow.
  • 仰天大笑 - He would laugh loudly at the sky, contrasting with his previous state of sorrow.
  • 忽跿跔踊跃，叫号痛哭 - Suddenly, he would leap and dance, shouting and weeping bitterly, describing erratic and intense emotional outbursts.
  • 或鼓腹高歌，或混舞于市 - Sometimes he would sing loudly with his belly, sometimes he would dance wildly in the market, showing his varied and unpredictable behavior.
  • 一日之间，颠态百出 - Within a day, he would display a hundred different deranged states, emphasizing the intensity and variety of his mental state.
  • 市人恶其扰，醉之酒，则颠止 - The people in the market disliked being disturbed by him, so they would get him drunk, and his derangement would stop, showing how his behavior affected others.
  • 岁余，病间，更号曰“个山” - After more than a year, his illness improved, and he changed his name to 'Ge Shan' (Piece Mountain), marking a transition in his mental state.
  • 吾为僧矣，何可不以驴名 - Since I am a monk, how can I not name myself after a donkey? A humorous and self-deprecating statement.
  • 遂更号曰“个山驴” - He then changed his name to 'Ge Shan Lu' (Piece Mountain Donkey), another self-chosen moniker.
  • 妻子俱死 - His wife and children all died, indicating a significant personal loss.
  • 斩先人祀，非所以为人后也 - Cutting off the ancestral worship is not what it means to be a descendant, a moral argument against abandoning family traditions.
  • 子无畏乎 - Are you not afraid? A rhetorical question challenging the previous statement.
  • 个山驴遂慨然蓄发谋妻子，号“八大山人” - Ge Shan Lu then resolutely grew his hair and planned to marry a wife, and he gave himself the name 'Ba Da Shan Ren' (Great Mountain Man), marking a significant personal transformation.
  • 八大者，四方四隅，皆我为大，而无大于我也 - Eight Great means that in all directions and corners, I am the greatest, and there is no one greater than me, a philosophical statement about self-importance and individuality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 甲申国亡 - The fall of the Ming Dynasty in 1644, a significant historical event.

  • 遂弃家为僧 - Becoming a monk was a common response to personal and national tragedy, often seen as a way to find spiritual solace.
  • 自号曰“雪个” - The name 'Xue Ge' (Snow Piece) is a poetic and symbolic choice, possibly reflecting a sense of purity or detachment.

  • 岁余，病间，更号曰“个山” - The name 'Ge Shan' (Piece Mountain) suggests a more grounded and stable state of mind.
  • 吾为僧矣，何可不以驴名 - The name 'Ge Shan Lu' (Piece Mountain Donkey) is a humorous and self-deprecating choice, possibly reflecting a sense of humility or self-mockery.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 人屋承父志，亦喑哑 - This sentence can be read as 'Rén Wū carried on his father's will, and he also became mute,' or 'Rén Wū, carrying on his father's will, also became mute.' The ambiguity lies in the relationship between the two clauses.
  • 左右承事者，皆语以目 - This sentence can be read as 'Those who served him on the left and right all communicated with him through gestures,' or 'Those who served him on the left and right all communicated with him using their eyes.' The ambiguity lies in the interpretation of '语以目.'
  • 对宾客寒暄以手 - This sentence can be read as 'He greeted guests with gestures,' or 'He greeted guests using his hands.' The ambiguity lies in the interpretation of '以手.'
  • 听人言古今事，心会处，则哑然笑 - This sentence can be read as 'When he heard people talk about ancient and modern events, if he understood them, he would laugh silently,' or 'When he heard people talk about ancient and modern events, if he understood them, he would laugh in a silent manner.' The ambiguity lies in the interpretation of '心会处' and '哑然笑.'
  • 遂弃家为僧，自号曰“雪个” - This sentence can be read as 'He then abandoned his family and became a monk, and he gave himself the name 'Xue Ge,' or 'He then abandoned his family and became a monk, giving himself the name 'Xue Ge.' The ambiguity lies in the relationship between the two clauses.
  • 未几病颠 - This sentence can be read as 'Not long after, he fell ill and became deranged,' or 'Not long after, he fell ill, and he became deranged.' The ambiguity lies in the relationship between the two clauses.
  • 市人恶其扰，醉之酒，则颠止 - This sentence can be read as 'The people in the market disliked being disturbed by him, so they would get him drunk, and his derangement would stop,' or 'The people in the market disliked being disturbed by him, so they would get him drunk, and his derangement would stop.' The ambiguity lies in the relationship between the two clauses.
  • 岁余，病间，更号曰“个山” - This sentence can be read as 'After more than a year, his illness improved, and he changed his name to 'Ge Shan,' or 'After more than a year, his illness improved, and he changed his name to 'Ge Shan.' The ambiguity lies in the relationship between the two clauses.
  • 吾为僧矣，何可不以驴名 - This sentence can be read as 'Since I am a monk, how can I not name myself after a donkey?' or 'Since I am a monk, how can I not name myself after a donkey?' The ambiguity lies in the relationship between the two clauses.

  • 至邃室 - To a secluded room; '邃' means 'deep' or 'secluded'.

  • 皆殊秀 - All are exceptionally beautiful; '殊' means 'exceptional', and '秀' means 'beautiful'.
  • 一淡妆者 - One who is lightly dressed; '淡妆' means 'lightly dressed' or 'lightly made up'.
  • 统诸美而先众音 - Leading all the beauties and preceding all the sounds; '统' means 'lead' or 'govern', '诸美' means 'all the beauties', and '先众音' means 'precede all the sounds'.
  • 情艳意娇 - Lovely and charming; '情' means 'emotion', '艳' means 'beautiful', '意' means 'intention' or 'mood', and '娇' means 'charming'.
  • 三桂不觉其神移心荡也 - Three Gui was unaware that his spirit was moved and his heart was agitated; '不觉' means 'unaware', '神移' means 'spirit moved', and '心荡' means 'heart agitated'.
  • 遽命解戎服，易轻裘 - Immediately ordered to remove his military attire and put on light clothing; '遽' means 'immediately', '解' means 'remove', '戎服' means 'military attire', and '易' means 'change', '轻裘' means 'light clothing'.
  • 顾谓畹曰 - Turned to Wan and said; '顾' means 'turn to', and '谓' means 'say'.
  • 此非所谓圆圆耶？ - Is this not what is called Yuan Yuan?; '所谓' means 'so-called' or 'what is called'.
  • 洵足倾人城矣！ - Indeed, it is enough to topple a city!; '洵' means 'indeed', '足' means 'enough', and '倾人城' means 'topple a city'.
  • 公宁勿畏而拥此耶？ - Are you not afraid to possess such beauty?; '宁' means 'are you not', '勿畏' means 'afraid', and '拥此' means 'possess such beauty'.

  • 卿乐甚？ - Are you enjoying yourself?; '卿' is a respectful term for 'you', and '乐甚' means 'enjoying yourself'.
  • 红拂尚不乐越公，矧不迨越公者耶？ - Red Brush still did not enjoy herself with Yue Gong, let alone those who are not as good as Yue Gong?; '红拂' is a historical figure, '尚不乐' means 'still did not enjoy', '越公' means 'Yue Gong', '矧' means 'let alone', and '不迨越公者' means 'those who are not as good as Yue Gong'.
  • 吴颔之 - Wu nodded; '颔' means 'nod'.
  • 酣饮间 - During the merry drinking; '酣' means 'merry', and '饮' means 'drinking'.
  • 警报踵至 - Warnings came one after another; '踵' means 'one after another'.
  • 吴似不欲行者，而不得不行 - Wu seemed reluctant to leave but had to leave; '似不欲行' means 'seemed reluctant to leave', and '不得不行' means 'had to leave'.
  • 设寇至，将奈何？ - If the enemy comes, what will we do?; '设' means 'if', '寇' means 'enemy', '至' means 'comes', and '将奈何' means 'what will we do'.
  • 能以圆圆见赠，吾当保公家，先于保国也 - If you can give me Yuan Yuan, I will protect your family first, before protecting the country; '见赠' means 'give me', '吾当保公家' means 'I will protect your family', and '先于保国也' means 'before protecting the country'.
  • 畹勉许之 - Wan reluctantly agreed; '勉' means 'reluctantly', and '许之' means 'agreed'.
  • 吴即命圆圆拜辞畹 - Wu immediately ordered Yuan Yuan to bow and bid farewell to Wan; '即命' means 'immediately ordered', '拜辞' means 'bow and bid farewell', and '畹' is the name of the person.
  • 择细马驮之去 - Chose a fine horse to carry her away; '择' means 'choose', '细马' means 'fine horse', and '驮之去' means 'carry her away'.
  • 畹爽然，无如何也 - Wan was greatly disappointed and could do nothing about it; '爽然' means 'greatly disappointed', and '无如何也' means 'could do nothing about it'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吳 - Refers to Wu Sangui, a prominent Ming general and later a Qing official.
  • 畹 - Refers to a high-ranking official or nobleman.
  • 圆圆 - Refers to a concubine named Yuan Yuan, who was highly favored by Wu Sangui.
  • 红拂 - Refers to a historical figure, a concubine of the Tang Dynasty general Li Jing (Yue Gong).
  • 越公 - Refers to Li Jing, a famous general of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 警报踵至 - Refers to the arrival of warnings or alarms, likely in a military context.
  • 寇 - Refers to the enemy or invaders, likely the Qing forces.
  • 保公家，先于保国也 - Reflects the Confucian value of prioritizing the protection of one's family over the state.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence indicates that Wu wanted to leave immediately after the wine was served.
  • This sentence shows that Wan repeatedly changed seats and eventually went to a secluded room.
  • This sentence describes the group of concubines playing musical instruments, all of whom were exceptionally beautiful.
  • This sentence highlights one concubine who was lightly dressed and led all the beauties and preceded all the sounds.
  • This sentence describes the concubine as lovely and charming, causing Wu to be unaware that his spirit was moved and his heart was agitated.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欣然受命 - Literally 'gladly accept the command,' indicating a positive and immediate response to a call or order.
  • 為闯籍矣 - Means 'has been registered by Chong,' where '闯' (Chong) refers to Li Zicheng, the leader of the peasant rebellion who later declared himself emperor.
  • 吾至当自还也 - Means 'I will return when I arrive,' expressing a determination to return home.
  • 为闯拘絷矣 - Means 'has been detained by Chong,' indicating capture or imprisonment by Li Zicheng's forces.
  • 吾至当即释也 - Means 'I will be released when I arrive,' expressing a belief that his father will be freed upon his arrival.
  • 为闯得之矣 - Means 'has been taken by Chong,' indicating that Teng's wife has been captured.
  • 拔剑砍案 - Literally 'draws his sword and chops the table,' a dramatic gesture indicating anger or determination.
  • 果有是，吾从若耶 - Means 'If it is true, will I follow you?' expressing a conditional willingness to act based on the truth of a situation.
  • 待罪戎行 - Literally 'awaiting punishment in military service,' indicating a sense of being in a position of potential blame or punishment while serving in the military.
  • 不意我国无人，望风而靡 - Means 'I did not expect our country to have no one to stand up, but everyone surrendered at the sight of the enemy,' expressing disappointment in the lack of resistance.
  • 侧闻圣主晏驾 - Means 'I have heard that the Holy Emperor has passed away,' a euphemism for the death of the emperor.
  • 不胜眦裂 - Literally 'cannot bear the tears in my eyes,' indicating intense grief or anger.
  • 吾父奋椎一击，誓不俱生 - Means 'My father would strike with a hammer and vow not to live together,' expressing a determination to fight to the death.
  • 刎颈以殉国难 - Means 'cut his own throat to die for the country's troubles,' indicating a willingness to die for the nation.
  • 隐忍偷生 - Means 'endure in silence and live secretly,' indicating a lack of courage or action.
  • 训以非义 - Means 'taught me something that is not right,' indicating moral or ethical instruction that is seen as incorrect.
  • 既无孝宽御寇之才 - Means 'neither has the talent of Xiao Kuang to defend against the enemy,' referring to a historical figure known for his military prowess.
  • 复愧平原骂贼之勇 - Means 'nor does he have the bravery of Pingyuan to curse the bandits,' referring to another historical figure known for his courage and defiance.
  • 父既不能为忠臣，儿安能为孝子乎 - Means 'If my father cannot be a loyal minister, how can I be a filial son?' expressing a sense of moral responsibility and duty.
  • 效秦庭之泣 - Means 'mimic the weeping of the Qin court,' a reference to a historical event where a person wept to express deep sorrow or appeal for help.
  • 乞王师以剿巨寇 - Means 'ask the imperial army to eliminate the great bandits,' indicating a request for military assistance.
  • 先败之于一片石 - Means 'defeat them first at Yipianshi,' indicating a specific location where a battle is planned.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又善作者龙吟 - Means 'also skilled in imitating the roar of a dragon', adding another dimension to the howler's abilities.
  • 醉卧大江滨，长吟数声，鱼虾皆破浪来朝，鼋鼍多迎涛以拜 - Means 'lying drunk by the great river, after a few long howls, fish and shrimp break the waves to pay homage, and turtles and alligators meet the tide to bow', describing a fantastical scene.
  • 他日，与黄鹤山樵、天都瞎汉，潇湘渔夫、虎头将军十数辈，登平山六一楼，拉啸翁啸 - Means 'one day, with the Yellow Crane Mountain Hermit, the Blind Man of Tiandu, the Fisher of Xiaoxiang, and the Tiger-Headed General and others, they climbed the Six-First Floor of Ping Mountain and pulled the howler to howl', setting the scene for the next event.
  • 啸翁以齿落固辞，强而后可 - Means 'the howler firmly refused due to tooth loss, but was forced to agree', showing the howler's reluctance.
  • 初发声，如空山铁笛，音韵悠扬 - Means 'at first, the sound was like a bamboo flute in an empty mountain, melodious and resonant', describing the initial sound.
  • 既而如鹤唳长天，声彻霄汉 - Means 'then it was like the cry of a crane in the vast sky, echoing through the heavens', describing the rising pitch and volume.
  • 少顷，移声向东，则风从西来，蒿莱尽伏，排闼击户，危楼欲动 - Means 'a moment later, shifting the sound eastward, the wind came from the west, flattening all vegetation, pushing doors and windows, and nearly shaking the tall building', describing the physical effects of the sound.
  • 再而移声向西，则风从东至，訚然荡然，如千军万马，驰骤于前，又若两军相角，短兵长剑紧接之势 - Means 'then shifting the sound westward, the wind came from the east, creating a calm and gentle effect, like a thousand troops and horses galloping, and as if two armies were clashing with short and long weapons', describing the changing effects.
  • 久之，则屋瓦欲飞，林木将拔也 - Means 'after a while, the tiles seemed to fly off, and the trees were about to be uprooted', emphasizing the intensity of the sound.
  • 于时炷香烬，而啸翁气竭，昏仆于地 - Means 'at that time, the incense burned out, and the howler fainted from exhaustion', describing the end of the event.
  • 众客大惊，亟呼山僧，灌以沸水，半晌乃苏 - Means 'the guests were greatly alarmed, quickly calling a mountain monk, who gave him boiling water, and he revived after a while', describing the aftermath.
  • 归而月印前溪矣 - Means 'they returned, and the moon reflected on the front stream', providing a peaceful conclusion.

  • 所贵乎道者，清净无为也 - 'What is valued in the Way is purity and non-action,' referring to Daoist principles.

  • 有声之声，延及百里；无声之声，延及四海 - 'The sound that can be heard extends for a hundred miles; the sound that cannot be heard extends for the entire world,' a Daoist metaphor for the power of the intangible.
  • 今汝所行，皆有为也 - 'Now everything you do is full of action,' criticizing the disciple's actions as contrary to Daoist ideals.

  • 三年，然后请见 - 'After three years, he then asked to be seen,' referring to the disciple's return after a period of reflection.
  • 子可以授吾大道矣 - 'You can now teach me the great Way,' a statement of mutual respect and recognition of the disciple's progress.

  • 吾将隐矣 - 'I will withdraw,' indicating the sage's decision to retreat from public life.
  • 掘一土穴山半，仅可容身 - 'Dug a hole in the middle of the mountain, just large enough to fit one person,' describing the sage's humble retreat.
  • 命以土掩，毋使有隙，但朝夕来呼吾可耳 - 'Ordered the earth to cover it, leaving no gaps, but allowing you to call me in the morning and evening,' indicating the sage's continued availability to his disciples.
  • 既埋，群弟子如命，朝夕往呼之 - 'After it was covered, the disciples followed orders, calling him in the morning and evening,' describing the disciples' continued care for the sage.
  • 活死人在土中，必大声应 - 'The living dead inside the earth would definitely respond loudly,' indicating the sage's continued presence and responsiveness.
  • 三年，呼之不应矣 - 'After three years, calling him no longer elicited a response,' indicating the sage's final departure.

  • 力而有德 - Describes the cow as strong and virtuous.
  • 日耕山田二十亩 - Indicates that the cow plows twenty acres of mountain fields daily.
  • 虽饥甚，不食田中苗 - States that even when very hungry, the cow does not eat the crops in the fields.
  • 吴宝之，令其十三岁子希年牧之 - Wu treasures the cow and lets his thirteen-year-old son, Xi Nian, tend to it.
  • 希年跨牛背，随牛所之 - Describes Xi Nian riding on the cow's back, following wherever the cow goes.
  • 牛方食草涧边，忽一虎从牛后林中出 - Describes the cow eating grass near a stream when suddenly a tiger emerges from the forest behind it.
  • 意欲攫希年 - The tiger intends to seize Xi Nian.
  • 牛知之，即旋身转向虎，徐行啮草 - The cow senses the danger and turns to face the tiger, slowly grazing as if unaware.
  • 希年惧，伏牛背不敢动 - Xi Nian is afraid and crouches on the cow's back, not daring to move.
  • 虎见牛来，且踞以俟 - The tiger sees the cow approaching and sits down, waiting.
  • 意相近即攫牛背儿也 - The tiger plans to grab Xi Nian from the cow's back.
  • 牛将迫虎，即遽奔以前，猛力触虎 - As the cow gets close to the tiger, it suddenly charges forward and forcefully collides with the tiger.
  • 虎方垂涎牛背儿，不及避，蹼而仰偃隘涧中，不能辗 - The tiger, still salivating over the prospect of grabbing Xi Nian, fails to dodge in time and is knocked onto its back in a narrow stream, unable to move.
  • 水壅浸虎首，虎毙 - The water rushes in, drowns the tiger, and kills it.
  • 希年驱牛返，白父，集众舁虎归，烹之 - Xi Nian drives the cow back home, informs his father, gathers the villagers, carries the tiger back, and cooks it.

  • 順治中，京口明經張行貞延為孺子句讀師 - During the Shunzhi era, Zhang Xingzhen, a scholar from Jingkou, invited him to be a teacher for his child.
  • 賓主甚相歡 - The host and guest were very happy together.
  • 他日飲青梅下 - One day, they drank under an apricot tree.

  • 噫！先生何云不難哉 - Ah! Why do you say it's not difficult?

  • 即使策骏马乘传，日夜兼程，行至此，亦槁矣 - Even if you ride a swift horse and travel day and night, by the time you get here, you would be exhausted.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 被贼断头者 - A person whose head was cut off by a thief; indicates severe injury.
  • 其子知其神 - His son knew his spirit; '神' (shén) refers to the soul or spiritual essence.
  • 祝巢夫，仙人也 - Direct address to someone named Zhuo Fu, calling him a 'fairy' or 'immortal'; '仙人' (xiān rén) is a term for immortals in Chinese mythology.
  • 速为我请来 - Quickly come and fetch him for me; '速' (sù) means 'quickly,' and '请来' (qǐng lái) means 'come and fetch.'
  • 郎君何妄也 - Young master, what foolishness is this?; '郎君' (láng jūn) is a respectful term for a young man, and '何妄' (hé wàng) means 'what foolishness.'
  • 颈不连项矣 - The neck is not connected to the neck anymore; emphasizes the severity of the injury.
  • 彼即有返魂丹 - If he has the pill of returning life; '返魂丹' (fǎn hún dān) is a mythical pill believed to restore life.
  • 乌能合即离之形骸哉 - How can it possibly combine what is already separated?; '乌能' (wū néng) is an interrogative particle meaning 'how can,' and '合即离' (hé jí lí) means 'combine what is already separated.'
  • 固强之而后行 - Insisted strongly and then proceeded; '固强' (gù qiáng) means 'insisted strongly,' and '而后行' (ér hòu xíng) means 'and then proceeded.'
  • 尧民抚其胸曰 - Yaomin patted his chest and said; '抚' (fǔ) means 'pat,' and '胸' (xiōng) means 'chest.'
  • 头虽断，身尚有暖气 - The head is severed, but the body still has warmth; '暖气' (nuǎn qì) means 'warmth.'
  • 有生气 - There is life force; '生气' (shēng qì) refers to the vital energy or life force.
  • 急以银针纫其头于项 - Quickly used silver needles to sew the head onto the neck; '纫' (rèn) means 'sew.'
  • 涂以末药一刀圭 - Applied a small amount of powder medicine; '末药' (mò yào) means 'powder medicine,' and '一刀圭' (yī dāo guī) is a unit of measurement for small quantities.
  • 熨以炭火 - Ironed with charcoal fire; '熨' (yùn) means 'iron,' and '炭火' (tàn huǒ) means 'charcoal fire.'
  • 煎人参汤，杂他药 - Boiled ginseng soup mixed with other medicines; '煎' (jiān) means 'boil,' and '杂他药' (zá tā yào) means 'mixed with other medicines.'
  • 启其齿灌之 - Opened his mouth and administered the medicine; '启' (qǐ) means 'open,' and '灌' (guàn) means 'administer.'
  • 须臾，则鼻微有息矣 - In a moment, there was a slight breath through the nose; '须臾' (xū yú) means 'in a moment,' and '鼻微有息' (bí wēi yǒu xī) means 'slight breath through the nose.'
  • 复以热酒灌之 - Again administered hot wine; '复' (fù) means 'again,' and '热酒' (rè jiǔ) means 'hot wine.'
  • 逾一昼夜 - More than one day and night; '逾' (yú) means 'more than,' and '一昼夜' (yī zhòu yè) means 'one day and night.'
  • 则出声矣 - Then made a sound; '出声' (chū shēng) means 'made a sound.'
  • 又一昼夜 - Another day and night.
  • 则呼其子而语矣 - Then called his son and spoke to him; '呼' (hū) means 'call,' and '语' (yǔ) means 'speak.'
  • 乃进以糜粥 - Then fed him porridge; '进' (jìn) means 'feed,' and '糜粥' (mí zhōu) means 'porridge.'
  • 七日而创合 - After seven days, the wound healed; '创合' (chuàng hé) means 'wound healed.'
  • 半月而如故 - After half a month, it returned to normal; '如故' (rú gù) means 'returned to normal.'
  • 举家拜谢 - The whole family bowed and thanked; '举家' (jǔ jiā) means 'the whole family,' and '拜谢' (bài xiè) means 'bowed and thanked.'
  • 愿以产之半酬之 - Willing to pay half of their property as a reward; '产之半' (chǎn zhī bàn) means 'half of their property,' and '酬之' (chóu zhī) means 'pay as a reward.'
  • 尧民不受 - Yaomin did not accept; '不受' (bù shòu) means 'did not accept.'
  • 后入终南山修道 - Later entered Zhongnan Mountain to cultivate Dao; '终南山' (zhōng nán shān) is a famous mountain range, and '修道' (xiū dào) means 'cultivate Dao.'
  • 不知所终 - Did not know his end; '不知' (bù zhī) means 'did not know,' and '所终' (suǒ zhōng) means 'his end.'
  • 无子，其术不传 - Had no son, so his skills were not passed on; '无子' (wú zǐ) means 'had no son,' and '其术不传' (qí shù bù chuán) means 'his skills were not passed on.'
  • 外史氏曰 - A formal title for the commentator or historian speaking; '外史氏' (wài shǐ shì) is a title for a historian or chronicler.
  • 世称华佗为神医 - The world calls Hua Tuo a divine physician; '世称' (shì chēng) means 'the world calls,' and '神医' (shén yī) means 'divine physician.'
  • 能破脑剜臂 - Can cut open the brain and remove the arm; '破脑' (pò nǎo) means 'cut open the brain,' and '剜臂' (wān bì) means 'remove the arm.'
  • 然未闻其能活既杀之人也 - But it has not been heard that he can revive those who have already been killed; '然' (rán) means 'but,' '未闻' (wèi wén) means 'has not been heard,' and '活既杀之人' (huó jì shā zhī rén) means 'revive those who have already been killed.'
  • 乃尧民能之 - But Yaomin can do it; '乃' (nǎi) means 'but,' and '能之' (néng zhī) means 'can do it.'
  • 不几远过于佗耶 - Is not far superior to Tuo?; '不几' (bù jǐ) means 'not far,' '远过' (yuǎn guò) means 'superior to,' and '于佗' (yú tuó) means 'Tuo.'
  • 孰谓后世无畸人哉 - Who would say there are no extraordinary people in later generations?; '孰谓' (shú wèi) means 'who would say,' '后世' (hòu shì) means 'later generations,' and '无畸人' (wú jī rén) means 'no extraordinary people.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 祝巢夫，仙人也 - Refers to someone named Zhuo Fu, called a 'fairy' or 'immortal'; '仙人' (xiān rén) is a term for immortals in Chinese mythology.
  • 世称华佗为神医 - The world calls Hua Tuo a divine physician; '神医' (shén yī) is a term for a highly skilled physician.

  • 《虞初志》《艳异编》 - Classical anthologies of strange tales and legends, compiled by various authors over centuries.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '逸興如落花依草，可補《虞初志》《艳异编》之所未備' combines a metaphor with a statement about the supplementing of classical anthologies. The metaphor '逸興如落花依草' sets the tone for the subsequent claim.
  • The sentence '文心九曲，幾欲占盡風流' uses a parallel structure to emphasize the depth and charm of the writer's mind.
  • The sentence '以愛花之心愛美人，領略定饒逸趣' uses a parallel structure to compare the love for flowers and women, highlighting the different pleasures derived from each.
  • The sentence '以愛美人之心愛花，護惜別有深情' contrasts the love for women and flowers, emphasizing the deeper emotional connection.
  • The sentence '丹麓惜花如命，固應有此奇遇' links the deep love for flowers to a special encounter, suggesting a logical connection.
  • The sentence '向讀《艳异》諸書，見花妖月姐，往往於文士有緣，心竊慕之，恨生平未之遇也' describes the narrator's past reading and his admiration for the supernatural beings, leading to a regretful sentiment.
  • The sentence '今讀此記，益令我神往矣' contrasts the past reading with the current reading, expressing a heightened sense of longing.
  • The sentence '孝犬，廣東東莞縣隱士陳恭隱家牝犬也，色白而尾骍，四足皆黑' introduces the filial dog, detailing its appearance and owner.
  • The sentence '恭隱痛父死國難，矢志不進取，隱居山中，以吟飲自縱，不與時人通' provides background on Tengongyin, explaining his reasons for living in seclusion.
  • The sentence '此犬隨恭隱，未嘗須臾離' describes the dog's constant presence with Tengongyin.
  • The sentence '每出，則犬先行數百步，若以為導者' describes the dog's behavior when Tengongyin goes out.
  • The sentence '遇豺狼蛇虎，則亟返，啮恭隱衣袂，曳之還，若不使前者' describes the dog's protective behavior when encountering dangerous animals.
  • The sentence '犬又隨後，離數十步，作大聲嗥，若以為衛者' describes the dog's continued protective behavior after Tengongyin returns.
  • The sentence '夜則於廬舍前後巡且吠，達旦不少休' describes the dog's nightly vigilance.

  • 解語識迦陵 - Parrot, a symbol of wisdom and understanding.
  • 續長恨歌 - Continuation of a long song of sorrow.
